title: Intensely Shattering Italian Guanciale Spaghetti Amatriciana
description: A floral, shattering, date-night Italian dish featuring rye bread and ferment, perfect for a romantic evening in a Korean brass bowl.
instructions: Cook spaghetti in a large pot of boiling, salted water until al dente, about 8 minutes.
In a large skillet, combine guanciale, onion, and garlic. Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the onion is translucent and the guanciale is crispy, about 10 minutes.
Add the anchovy, tomato paste, and red wine to the skillet. Cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce is thick and syrupy, about 5 minutes.
Add the cooked spaghetti to the skillet, tossing to combine with the sauce. Season with black pepper to taste.
Transfer the spaghetti to a Korean brass bowl and top with parmesan, parsley, and rye bread. Serve immediately.
If desired, sprinkle with korean chili flakes for added heat.
Garnish with additional parsley and serve immediately.
Serve with a side of rye bread for dipping in the sauce.
Optional: add a small dollop of ferment, such as kimchi or sauerkraut, on top of the spaghetti for added flavor and nutrition.
Optional: serve with a side of roasted vegetables, such as broccoli or carrots, for added nutrition and flavor.
